AsyncExtractRequest.description:
Request model for async extract endpoint.

Extends ExtractRequest with output_save_url for ZDR support.

markdown.description: The Markdown file or Markdown content to extract data from.
markdown.title: Markdown

markdown_url.description: The URL to the Markdown file to extract data from.
markdown_url.title: Markdown Url

model.description: The version of the model to use for extraction. Use `extract-latest` to use the latest version.
model.title: Model

output_save_url.description: If zero data retention (ZDR) is enabled, you must enter a URL for the extracted output to be saved to. When ZDR is enabled, the extracted content will not be in the API response.
output_save_url.title: Output Save Url

schema.description: JSON schema for field extraction. This schema determines what key-values pairs are extracted from the Markdown. The schema must be a valid JSON object and will be validated before processing the document.
schema.title: Schema

strict.description: If True, reject schemas with unsupported fields (HTTP 422). If False, prune unsupported fields and continue. Only applies to extract versions that support schema validation.
strict.title: Strict

AsyncExtractRequest.title: AsyncExtractRequest

custom_prompts.description: Optional JSON string mapping chunk types to custom parsing prompts. Only the `figure` key is supported, for example '{"figure":"Describe axis labels in detail."}'.
custom_prompts.title: Custom Prompts

document.description: A file to be parsed. The file can be a PDF or an image. See the list of supported file types here: https://docs.landing.ai/ade/ade-file-types. Either this parameter or the `document_url` parameter must be provided.
document.title: Document

document_url.description: The URL to the file to be parsed. The file can be a PDF or an image. See the list of supported file types here: https://docs.landing.ai/ade/ade-file-types. Either this parameter or the `document` parameter must be provided.
document_url.title: Document Url

model.description: The version of the model to use for parsing.
model.title: Model

output_save_url.description: If zero data retention (ZDR) is enabled, you must enter a URL for the parsed output to be saved to. When ZDR is enabled, the parsed content will not be in the API response.
output_save_url.title: Output Save Url

password.description: Password for encrypted document files. If the document is password-protected, provide the password to decrypt and process the document. Ignored for unencrypted documents.
password.title: Password

split.description: If you want to split documents into smaller sections, include the split parameter. Set the parameter to page to split documents at the page level. The splits object in the API output will contain a set of data for each page.

warnings.description: Structured warnings from the extraction process. Each warning is an instance of ExtractWarning with 'code' (e.g. 'nonconformant_schema') and 'msg' (human-readable description). Present only for extract versions from extract-20260314 and above that support structured warnings.
warnings.title: Warnings

markdown_urls.description: URLs to Markdown files to analyze for schema generation.
markdown_urls.title: Markdown Urls

markdowns.description: Markdown files or inline content strings to analyze for schema generation. Multiple documents can be provided for better schema coverage.
markdowns.title: Markdowns

model.description: The version of the model to use for schema generation. Use `extract-latest` to use the latest version.
model.title: Model

prompt.description: Instructions for how to generate or modify the schema.
prompt.title: Prompt

schema.description: Existing JSON schema to iterate on or refine.
schema.title: Schema

extraction_schema.description: The generated JSON schema as a string.
extraction_schema.title: Extraction Schema

metadata.description: The metadata for the schema generation process.

suggested_class.description: Proposed class when the prediction is 'unknown'.
suggested_class.title: Suggested Class

ClassifyClass.description: A single classification option: a class name plus optional description.

description.description: Detailed description of what this class represents.
description.title: Description

ClassifyClass.title: ClassifyClass

classes.description: The possible classes that can be assigned to pages in the document. Each entry is an object with a `class` name and an optional `description`. Only one class is assigned per page; unclassifiable pages receive 'unknown'. Can be provided as a JSON string in form data.
classes.title: Classes

document.description: A file to be classified. Either this parameter or the `document_url` parameter must be provided.
document.title: Document

document_url.description: The URL of the document to be classified. Either this parameter or the `document` parameter must be provided.
document_url.title: Document Url

model.description: Classification model version. Defaults to the latest.
model.title: Model

ClassifyResponse.description: Response model for the classify endpoint.
ClassifyResponse.title: ClassifyResponse

CustomPromptKey.description: Chunk types that support custom prompts.
CustomPromptKey.title: CustomPromptKey

CustomPrompts.description: Map of chunk type to custom prompt string.
CustomPrompts.title: CustomPrompts

ExtractJobStatusResponse.description: The status of an extract job, plus the results once it completes.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the job was created.
created_at.title: Created At

data.description: The extraction results, returned here when the job is complete and you did not set an `output_save_url`. Large results are returned through `output_url` instead.

failure_reason.description: If the job failed, a message describing what went wrong.
failure_reason.title: Failure Reason

job_id.description: A unique identifier for this extract job.
job_id.title: Job Id

metadata.description: Information about the extraction, such as the model version, duration, credit usage, and any schema warnings.

output_url.description: A URL to download the extraction results. Provided when the job is complete and either you set an `output_save_url` or the result is larger than 1 MB. URLs for large results are temporary and expire one hour after you request the job.
output_url.title: Output Url

progress.description: Job completion. Either 0.0 (not yet complete) or 1.0 (complete).
progress.title: Progress

received_at.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the job was received.
received_at.title: Received At

status.description: The current state of the job: `pending`, `processing`, `completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`.
status.title: Status

version.description: The exact model snapshot used for the extraction.
version.title: Version

ExtractJobStatusResponse.title: ExtractJobStatusResponse

fallback_model_version.description: The extract model that was actually used to extract the data when the initial extraction attempt failed with the requested version.
fallback_model_version.title: Fallback Model Version

schema_violation_error.description: A detailed error message shows why the extracted data does not fully conform to the input schema. Null means the extraction result is consistent with the input schema.
schema_violation_error.title: Schema Violation Error

warnings.description: Structured warnings from the extraction process. Each warning is an instance of ExtractWarning with 'code' (e.g. 'nonconformant_schema') and 'msg' (human-readable description). Present only for extract versions from extract-20260314 and above that support structured warnings.
warnings.title: Warnings

markdown.description: The Markdown file or Markdown content to extract data from.
markdown.title: Markdown

markdown_url.description: The URL to the Markdown file to extract data from.
markdown_url.title: Markdown Url

model.description: The version of the model to use for extraction. Use `extract-latest` to use the latest version.
model.title: Model

schema.description: JSON schema for field extraction. This schema determines what key-values pairs are extracted from the Markdown. The schema must be a valid JSON object and will be validated before processing the document.
schema.title: Schema

strict.description: If True, reject schemas with unsupported fields (HTTP 422). If False, prune unsupported fields and continue. Only applies to extract versions that support schema validation.
strict.title: Strict

extraction_metadata.description: The extracted key-value pairs and the chunk_reference for each one.
extraction_metadata.title: Extraction Metadata

metadata.description: The metadata for the extraction process.

code.description: The type of warning, used to translate to a status code downstream

msg.description: Human-readable description of the warning with more details
msg.title: Msg

JobStatusResponse.description: Unified response for job status endpoint.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) for when the job was created. Mirrors received_at; exposed so clients have an explicit creation time.
created_at.title: Created At

data.description: The parsed output (ParseResponse for documents, SpreadsheetParseResponse for spreadsheets), if the job is complete and the `output_save_url` parameter was not used.
data.title: Data

output_url.description: The URL to the parsed content. This field contains a URL when the job is complete and either you specified the `output_save_url` parameter or the result is larger than 1MB. When the result exceeds 1MB, the URL is a presigned S3 URL that expires after 1 hour. Each time you GET the job, a new presigned URL is generated.
output_url.title: Output Url

progress.description: Job completion progress as a decimal from 0 to 1, where 0 is not started, 1 is finished, and values between 0 and 1 indicate work in progress.
progress.title: Progress

JobStatusResponse.title: JobStatusResponse

JobSummary.description: Summary of a job for listing.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) for when the job was created. Mirrors received_at; exposed so clients have an explicit creation time.
created_at.title: Created At

progress.description: Job completion as a decimal from 0 (not started) to 1 (complete).
progress.title: Progress

JobSummary.title: JobSummary

custom_prompts.description: Optional JSON string mapping chunk types to custom parsing prompts. Only the `figure` key is supported, for example '{"figure":"Describe axis labels in detail."}'.
custom_prompts.title: Custom Prompts

document.description: A file to be parsed. The file can be a PDF or an image. See the list of supported file types here: https://docs.landing.ai/ade/ade-file-types. Either this parameter or the `document_url` parameter must be provided.
document.title: Document

document_url.description: The URL to the file to be parsed. The file can be a PDF or an image. See the list of supported file types here: https://docs.landing.ai/ade/ade-file-types. Either this parameter or the `document` parameter must be provided.
document_url.title: Document Url

model.description: The version of the model to use for parsing.
model.title: Model

password.description: Password for encrypted document files. If the document is password-protected, provide the password to decrypt and process the document. Ignored for unencrypted documents.
password.title: Password

split.description: If you want to split documents into smaller sections, include the split parameter. Set the parameter to page to split documents at the page level. The splits object in the API output will contain a set of data for each page.

guidelines.description: Natural-language instructions to control hierarchy. Examples: 'Group by topic', 'Treat each numbered section as a top-level entry'.
guidelines.title: Guidelines

markdown.description: Parsed markdown with reference anchors (). This is the markdown field from a parse response.
markdown.title: Markdown

markdown_url.description: URL to fetch the markdown from.
markdown_url.title: Markdown Url

model.description: Section model version. Defaults to latest.
model.title: Model

SectionTOCEntry.description: A single entry in the flat table of contents.
SectionTOCEntry.title: SectionTOCEntry

description.description: Detailed description of what this split type represents
description.title: Description

identifier.description: Identifier to partition/group the splits by
identifier.title: Identifier

name.description: Name of the split classification type
name.title: Name

SplitData.description: Split data for split classification endpoint.
SplitData.title: SplitData

version.description: Model version used for split classification
version.title: Version

SplitRequest.description: Request model for split classification endpoint.

markdown.description: The Markdown file or Markdown content to split.
markdown.title: Markdown

markdown_url.description: The URL to the Markdown file to split.
markdown_url.title: Markdown Url

model.description: Model version to use for split classification. Defaults to the latest version.
model.title: Model

split_class.description: List of split classification options/configuration. Can be provided as JSON string in form data.
split_class.title: Split Class

SplitRequest.title: SplitRequest

SplitResponse.description: Response model for split classification endpoint.
SplitResponse.title: SplitResponse

SpreadsheetChunk.description:
Chunk from spreadsheet parsing.

Can represent:
- Table chunks from spreadsheet cells
- Parsed content chunks from embedded images (text, table, figure, etc.)

grounding.description: Visual grounding coordinates from /parse API (only for chunks derived from embedded images)

id.description: Chunk ID - format: '{sheet_name}-{cell_range}' for tables, '{sheet_name}-image-{index}-{anchor_cell}-chunk-{i}-{type}' for parsed image chunks
id.title: Id

markdown.description: Chunk content as HTML table with anchor tag (for tables) or parsed markdown content (for chunks from images)
markdown.title: Markdown

type.description: Chunk type: 'table' for spreadsheet tables, or types from /parse (text, table, figure, form, etc.) for chunks derived from embedded images
type.title: Type

SpreadsheetChunk.title: SpreadsheetChunk

total_cells.description: Total non-empty cells across all sheets
total_cells.title: Total Cells

SpreadsheetParseResponse.description:
Response from /ade/parse-spreadsheet endpoint.

Similar structure to ParseResponse but without grounding.

markdown.description: Full document as HTML with anchor tags and tables
markdown.title: Markdown

SpreadsheetParseResponse.title: SpreadsheetParseResponse

SpreadsheetSplit.description:
Sheet-based split from spreadsheet parsing.

Similar to ParseSplit but grouped by sheet instead of page.
Supports both 'page' (per-sheet) and 'full' (all sheets) split types.

class.description: Split class: 'page' for per-sheet splits, 'full' for single split with all content
class.title: Class

identifier.description: Split identifier: sheet name for 'page' splits, 'full' for full split
identifier.title: Identifier

sheets.description: Sheet indices: single element for 'page' splits, all indices for 'full' split
sheets.title: Sheets

SpreadsheetSplit.title: SpreadsheetSplit

Basic Auth.description:
Your unique API key for authentication.

Get your API key here: https://va.landing.ai/settings/api-key.

If using the EU endpoint, get your API key here: https://va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/settings/api-key.

info.description: OpenAPI schema for ADE endpoints only
info.title: ADE API

post.description:
Classify the pages of a document into classes you define.

This endpoint accepts PDFs, images, and other supported file types
(either as a `document` upload or `document_url`) together with a
list of `classes`, and returns a classification result for each page.

For EU users, use this endpoint:

`https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/classify`.

Extract structured data from Markdown using a JSON schema.

This endpoint
    processes Markdown content and extracts structured data according to the provided
    JSON schema.

For EU users, use this endpoint:


    `https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/extract`.

206.description: Extraction completed with success but there was a schema validation error.

post.description:
Generate a JSON schema from Markdown using AI.

This endpoint analyzes Markdown
    content and generates a JSON schema suitable for use with the extract endpoint.
    It can also refine an existing schema based on new documents or iterate on a schema
    based on prompt instructions.

For EU users, use this endpoint:


    `https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/extract/build-schema`.

get.description:
List all async extract jobs associated with your API key.

Returns the list of jobs or an error response. For EU users, use this endpoint:

`https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/extract/jobs`.

post.description:
Extract structured data asynchronously.

This endpoint creates a job that handles the processing for large markdown
documents.

For EU users, use this endpoint:

`https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/extract/jobs`.

get.description:
Get the status for an async extract job.

Returns the job status or an error
   response. For EU users, use this endpoint:


   `https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/extract/jobs/{job_id}`.

206.description: Extraction completed with success but there was a schema validation error.

post.description:
Parse a document or spreadsheet.

This endpoint parses documents (PDF, images)
    and spreadsheets (XLSX, CSV) into structured Markdown, chunks, and metadata.
    

 For EU users, use this endpoint:


    `https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/parse`.

206.description: There were some pages that failed to be parsed.

get.description:
List all async parse jobs associated with your API key. Returns the list of jobs
or an error response. For EU users, use this endpoint:


`https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/parse/jobs`.

post.description:
Parse documents asynchronously.

This endpoint creates a job that handles the
    processing for both large documents and large batches of documents.

 For EU
    users, use this endpoint:


    `https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/parse/jobs`.

get.description:
Get the status for an async parse job.

Returns the job status or an error
   response. For EU users, use this endpoint:


   `https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/parse/jobs/{job_id}`.

206.description: There were some pages that failed to be parsed.

post.description:
Section parsed markdown into a hierarchical table of contents.

This endpoint accepts the markdown output from /ade/parse
(with reference anchors) and returns a flat, reading-order list of
sections with hierarchy levels and reference ranges.

For EU users, use this endpoint:

`https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/section`.

Split classification for documents.

This endpoint classifies document sections
    based on markdown content and split options.

For EU users, use this endpoint:


    `https://api.va.eu-west-1.landing.ai/v1/ade/split`.
